overview: Drive the existing pySTAMPS codebase to a state where the full parity validation loop completes without interruption and proves parity on the required datasets. The PRD makes the uninterrupted audit loop the primary product objective and treats packaging, docs, and validation-gate hygiene as supporting work required to keep the audit green.
goals: Make the full parity validation audit complete end-to-end without manual intervention or partial restarts.
Eliminate blocking parity mismatches until the required datasets pass the audit and verify gates.
Ensure docs, scripts, and tests describe and enforce the real validation contract for the standalone repository.
nonGoals: Adding new product features unrelated to parity, validation, or audit reliability.
Changing dataset contents, golden artifacts, or expected outputs to force a passing audit.
Broad refactors outside the failing parity path unless required to remove a verified blocker.
successMetrics: The required audit command exits with code 0 and writes a complete latest_audit.json without manual intervention.
The required verify command exits with code 0 for the required golden comparison dataset copy.
All required quality gate commands pass on the implementation branch before handoff.
Contributor and release docs reference the exact commands and constraints needed to keep the parity loop green.

stack.framework: Python package and CLI for existing pySTAMPS repository
stack.hosting: Local developer/runtime execution; no web hosting required
stack.runtime: uv-managed Python environment with local filesystem datasets and optional external tools

routes.name: Pipeline Run CLI
routes.purpose: Execute staged processing on a dataset copy for parity validation.

routes.name: Verify CLI
routes.purpose: Compare generated artifacts against the golden dataset and fail on any mismatch.

routes.name: Audit Driver
routes.purpose: Run the required parity loop across the required datasets and emit the machine-readable audit result.

uiNotes: This project has no browser UI; the public interaction surface is CLI plus machine-readable JSON artifacts.
Validation output must be legible in terminal JSON and on-disk audit artifacts because the audit must run unattended.
Docs must clearly distinguish between fresh-clone unit validation and local-dataset parity validation.

importFormat.description: Local filesystem datasets discovered from required inputs_and_outputs directories; audit output is JSON emitted by scripts/validate_audit.py.

rules: The full parity validation loop is the main objective of the program and takes precedence over ancillary cleanup.
The validation audit must run completely without interruptions, manual restarts, or partial artifact recovery.
Any non-zero exit code, missing required artifact, partial audit JSON, or manual retry requirement is a validation-gate failure.
Parity must be ensured against the required local golden datasets; changing the golden data or expected outputs to force success is forbidden.
Local validation datasets remain untracked repository assets; stories must make the audit workflow reliable without requiring those datasets to be committed.
Every parity fix must be validated with the exact top-level quality gate commands before handoff.
If upstream stage residuals block downstream parity, the implementation must expand scope to the minimum upstream stage set needed to make the full loop green.
Docs must be updated whenever commands, validation gates, audit behavior, or dataset expectations change.
Generated artifacts in dist/ or build/ must not be treated as source of truth for behavior changes.
qualityGates: uv run --with twine python -m twine check dist/*

OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=1 OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 MKL_NUM_THREADS=1 PYTHONPATH=. uv run python scripts/validate_audit.py --datasets inputs_and_outputs/InSAR_dataset_test_stage8diag inputs_and_outputs/InSAR_dataset_test --output inputs_and_outputs/validation_runs/latest_audit.json
OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=1 OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 MKL_NUM_THREADS=1 PYTHONPATH=. uv run pystamps verify --run <run-copy> --golden ./inputs_and_outputs/InSAR_dataset_test

stories.title: Stabilize the audit entrypoint and validation contract
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want a single audit driver and explicit contract so the full parity loop can run unattended and fail deterministically when requirements are not met.
acceptanceCriteria: The repository has one supported audit entrypoint that executes the required full validation loop and writes the required JSON artifact.
The audit entrypoint validates that all required local datasets exist before work begins and fails fast with a clear missing-dataset report when they do not.
The audit result includes enough structured detail to determine whether the loop completed, which workflows failed, and whether any interruption occurred.
Example: with both required datasets present, the audit command runs through all required workflows and writes latest_audit.json with contract, audits, failed_workflows, and ok fields.
Negative case: if one required dataset path is missing, the audit command exits non-zero and reports the missing dataset instead of hanging, partially succeeding, or silently skipping it.

stories.title: Make standalone validation gates truthful and reproducible
stories.description: As a contributor, I want the docs, tests, and packaging surface to reflect the real validation contract so the audit gate can be run correctly from a clean source checkout.
acceptanceCriteria: Tracked docs reference the exact validation and packaging commands that exist in the standalone repository.
Dataset-backed tests are explicitly optional or guarded so fresh-clone validation does not pretend local parity datasets are committed.
Package metadata and manifests exclude generated or recursive release artifacts that would corrupt the release validation surface.
Example: a contributor following the updated docs can run unit validation and packaging validation without guessing a Makefile or hidden CI surface.
Negative case: docs must not reference nonexistent task runners, silently ignored config keys, or fallback behaviors that the code does not implement.

stories.title: Reproduce and classify the full failing parity loop
stories.description: As a parity engineer, I want a current, reproducible classification of all remaining full-loop failures so fixes can target the real blocking stages rather than stale assumptions.
acceptanceCriteria: The required audit and verify commands are run on the current branch against the required datasets and their outputs are recorded.
Failures are grouped by producing stage or stage cluster with concrete artifact names and mismatch keys.
The story identifies whether full-loop parity is blocked by upstream stages, merged unwrap stages, downstream statistics stages, or a combination.
Example: the audit output explicitly distinguishes stage 3-4 residuals from stage 5-6 unwrap drift and stage 7-8 downstream mismatches.
Negative case: it is not acceptable to declare parity work complete based on stale artifacts, skipped stages, or a partial rerun that leaves existing outputs untouched.

stories.title: Eliminate stage 5-6 blockers that prevent full-loop parity
stories.description: As a parity engineer, I want the merged unwrap and smoothing path to match the golden reference closely enough that downstream stage 7-8 failures are no longer masked by upstream drift.
acceptanceCriteria: Any confirmed stage 5-6 root-cause mismatch identified by the audit is fixed in source-of-truth Python code and covered by the smallest meaningful regression tests.
After applying the fix, the required verify command shows that the targeted stage 5-6 artifacts improve or fully pass, with before/after evidence captured in the audit loop.
If a suspected fix does not improve parity, it is reverted and documented rather than left in place as speculative drift.
Example: a reproduced `phuw2.mat` or `uw_interp.mat` mismatch is fixed, rerun on a clean dataset copy, and verified to reduce the exact failing key(s).
Negative case: do not change stage 7-8 code to paper over unchanged stage 5-6 mismatches when the audit still attributes the blocker to unwrap/smoothing artifacts.

stories.title: Resolve stage 7-8 numerical parity only after upstream blockers are contained
stories.description: As a parity engineer, I want the stage 7-8 numerical outputs to match the golden reference once upstream blockers are contained, so the full loop can reach a true green state.
acceptanceCriteria: Stage 7-8 fixes are driven by refreshed audit evidence from current reruns, not by stale assumptions or unsupported Matlab inferences.
Each numerical fix must show concrete before/after movement on the failing keys in `scla2.mat`, `mean_v.mat`, or `uw_space_time.mat`.
If a proposed numerical fix does not reduce the targeted mismatch, it must be reverted before story completion.
Example: a stage 7 solve-path fix reduces the reported max_abs or wrapped_max_abs on the exact failing keys and leaves packaging/unit gates green.
Negative case: a change that keeps `C_ps_uw` flat, worsens `mean_v.mat.m`, or shifts drift without reducing the full-loop failure count does not satisfy the story.

stories.title: Pass the uninterrupted full parity loop and lock the gate
stories.description: As a release owner, I want the full parity loop to pass end-to-end without interruption so the audit validation gate is satisfied and documented as the required release standard.
acceptanceCriteria: All top-level quality gate commands pass on the final branch state.
The required audit command completes end-to-end without manual intervention and writes a successful latest_audit.json with no failed workflows.
The required verify command exits 0 on the required run copy versus the golden dataset.
Updated docs describe the exact passing workflow, the required local assets, and the rule that any interruption or partial run is a validation failure.
Example: from a clean local checkout with the required datasets available, a maintainer can execute the documented commands in order and obtain a green audit artifact and green verify result.
Negative case: if any command requires a manual restart, leaves stale outputs in place, or exits non-zero, the validation gate is not passed and the story remains open.
